intro: Drimia anomala, commonly known as the "sickle bulb", is a medicinal plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Drimia anomala include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
